text: 6 Minute English From bbclearningenglish .com
zh: [0] 6分钟英语 来自bbclearningenglish.com

text: Hello. This is 6 Minute English from BBC Learning English.
zh: [1] 大家好,欢迎收听BBC英语教学的《6分钟英语》节目。

text: I'm Phil. And I'm Beth. Do you like honey, Phil? I use it a lot in cooking, so yes.
zh: [2] 我是Phil。我是Beth。Phil,你喜欢蜂蜜吗?我做饭经常用蜂蜜,所以是的,我喜欢。

text: Well, the relationship between humans, honey, and the bees which make it goes back thousands of years.
zh: [3] 人类、蜂蜜以及酿造蜂蜜的蜜蜂之间的关系可以追溯到几千年前。

text: Prized for its taste and medicinal properties, in ancient times honey was known as liquid gold.
zh: [4] 蜂蜜因其味道和药用价值而备受推崇,在古代被称为“液体黄金”。

text: But now, thanks to modern farming methods, population of many insects, including bees, are in decline.
zh: [5] 但如今,由于现代农业方式的影响,包括蜜蜂在内的许多昆虫的数量正在减少。

text: Saoirse, a worried listener from London, called BBC World Service programme CrowdScience about the problem.
zh: [6] 来自伦敦的听众Saoirse对此感到担忧,她打电话给BBC世界服务的节目《CrowdScience》,提出了这个问题。

text: I'm Saoirse from the UK and my question is, can honey help save bees?
zh: [7] 我是来自英国的Saoirse,我的问题是,吃蜂蜜能帮助拯救蜜蜂吗?

text: I've been vegan for about nine years and I don't eat honey myself,
zh: [8] 我吃素已经大约九年了,自己不吃蜂蜜,

text: but I still have a question mark around it because I know that our bee populations are
zh: [9] 但我心里一直有个疑问,因为我知道我们的蜜蜂数量正在

text: declining. Saoirse is a vegan, someone who doesn't eat anything which comes from animals.
zh: [10] 减少。Saoirse是一名严格素食者,也就是不吃任何动物制品的人。

text: Not just meat, but things like milk, eggs and honey too.
zh: [11] 不仅是肉类,像牛奶、鸡蛋和蜂蜜这些也不吃。

text: Although Saoirse doesn't eat honey herself, she says there's a question mark around eating
zh: [12] 虽然Saoirse自己不吃蜂蜜,但她说对于吃蜂蜜这件事,

text: honey. The phrase a question mark around something means there's doubt or uncertainty about something.
zh: [13] 她心里存有疑问。短语“a question mark around something”意思是对某事存在疑虑或不确定。

text: And what Saoirse is uncertain about is whether eating honey might actually save bees.
zh: [14] 而Saoirse不确定的是,吃蜂蜜是否真的能拯救蜜蜂。

text: So would it be better for declining bee populations if more people ate honey?
zh: [15] 那么,如果更多人吃蜂蜜,对于日益减少的蜜蜂数量来说,是否会更好呢?

text: That's what we'll be discussing using some useful new words and
zh: [16] 这就是我们今天要讨论的内容,我们会用到一些实用的新单词和

text: phrases. Plus you'll find all the vocabulary from this episode on our website, bbclearningenglish .com.
zh: [17] 短语。此外,你还可以在我们的网站bbclearningenglish.com上找到本期节目的所有词汇。

text: But before that, I have a question for you,
zh: [18] 不过在那之前,我有一个问题想问你,

text: Phil. The buzzing sound bees are famous for is made by flapping their four wings incredibly fast.
zh: [19] Phil。蜜蜂闻名的嗡嗡声是由它们极快地扇动四只翅膀发出的。

text: But how fast? Do honeybees flap their wings a 90 times per second
zh: [20] 但到底有多快呢?蜜蜂扇动翅膀的频率是a 每秒90次,

text: b 190 times per second or c 290 times per second?
zh: [21] b 每秒190次,还是c 每秒290次?

text: Wow, I have absolutely no idea, so I'm going to guess 190 times per second.
zh: [22] 哇,我完全不知道,那我就猜每秒190次吧。

text: OK, well we'll find out the answer later in the programme. We've been talking about honeybees,
zh: [23] 好的,我们稍后会在节目中揭晓答案。我们一直在谈论蜜蜂,

text: but these are just one of the 20 ,000 species of bee which exist around the
zh: [24] 但它们只是全世界2万种蜜蜂中的一种。

text: world. In fact, honeybees are one of the most widespread insects on the planet, and not endangered at all.
zh: [25] 事实上,蜜蜂是地球上分布最广的昆虫之一,一点也不濒危。

text: But the picture isn't so good for wild bees,
zh: [26] 但对于野生蜜蜂来说,情况就没那么乐观了,

text: including the bumblebee, as biologist Dave Goulson from the University of Sussex explained
zh: [27] 包括大黄蜂在内,正如萨塞克斯大学的生物学家Dave Goulson向

text: to BBC World Service programme CrowdScience.
zh: [28] BBC世界服务节目《CrowdScience》解释的那样。

text: DAVE GOULSON, University of Sussex, In the UK, we have 26 bumblebee species, but three have gone extinct.
zh: [29] 萨塞克斯大学的Dave Goulson说:在英国,我们有26种大黄蜂,但其中三种已经灭绝。

text: Many of the others are now clinging on in little isolated pockets of good habitat.
zh: [30] 许多其他种类现在只能在一些孤立的优质栖息地里勉强维持生存。

text: Honeybees have beekeepers to look after them, but nobody's looking out for the wild bees.
zh: [31] 蜜蜂有养蜂人照顾,但没有人在照看野生蜜蜂。

text: Three of the 26 species of British bumblebee have gone extinct.
zh: [32] 英国26种大黄蜂中有三种已经灭绝。

text: And Dave says other species are clinging on, meaning barely continuing to survive with great effort.
zh: [33] Dave说其他物种正在“clinging on”,意思是靠着极大的努力勉强维持生存。

text: This happens in isolated pockets, small areas of habitat in which a particular insect or animal can survive.
zh: [34] 这种情况发生在“孤立的栖息地”中,也就是某种昆虫或动物能够生存的小片栖息地。

text: So while honeybees may not be endangered, lots of other bee species are.
zh: [35] 所以,尽管蜜蜂本身可能不算濒危,但其他许多蜂类物种却面临危险。

text: And this is bad news because we rely on insects to pollinate make crops and to keep ecosystems functioning.
zh: [36] 这是个坏消息,因为我们依靠昆虫来为农作物授粉,并维持生态系统的正常运转。

text: That's right Beth, 87 % of all plant species need insects for pollination.
zh: [37] 没错,Beth,87%的植物物种都需要昆虫来完成授粉。

text: So without them, most plants on Earth would disappear, which would obviously be catastrophic.
zh: [38] 所以如果没有它们,地球上大多数植物都会消失,这显然将是灾难性的。

text: But according to Dave Goulson, pollination is just one of the many things bees do for
zh: [39] 但据Dave Goulson所说,授粉只是蜜蜂为我们做的众多事情之一,

text: us, as he explained to BBC World Service's CrowdScience.
zh: [40] 正如他向BBC世界服务的《CrowdScience》所解释的那样。

text: That isn't the only thing that insects are doing and there are much less glamorous,
zh: [41] 昆虫做的远不止授粉这一件事,还有很多不那么“光鲜”、

text: less well -known things like recycling cow poo
zh: [42] 也不太为人所知的工作,比如分解牛粪、

text: and helping to keep the soil healthy and helping to control crop pests and so on.
zh: [43] 帮助保持土壤健康、帮助控制农作物害虫等等。

text: All of which are also really important and we kind of take them for granted or are just oblivious that they're even happening.
zh: [44] 所有这些都非常重要,但我们往往对此习以为常,甚至完全没有意识到它们的存在。

text: Recycling cow poo and keeping the soil healthy are two important jobs bees do which
zh: [45] 分解牛粪和维持土壤健康是蜜蜂所做的两项重要工作,而我们常常

text: we take for granted. If you take something for granted, you fail to value or appreciate something which benefits you.
zh: [46] 对此不以为然。如果你对某件事“take for granted”,意思是你没有珍视或感激那些对你有益的事情。

text: We humans are oblivious, meaning unaware or unmindful of the many things bees do to support life on Earth.
zh: [47] 而我们人类对蜜蜂为维持地球生命所做的许多事情“oblivious”,也就是浑然不觉、毫无意识。

text: So while eating more honey probably won't do much to save wild bees,
zh: [48] 所以,虽然多吃蜂蜜可能对拯救野生蜜蜂帮助不大,

text: there are things you can do, such as growing bee -friendly plants which flower in spring, like lavender.
zh: [49] 但你还是可以做一些事情,比如种植像薰衣草这样在春天开花、对蜜蜂友好的植物。

text: OK Phil, I think it's time to reveal the answer to my question.
zh: [50] 好了Phil,我想是时候揭晓我问题的答案了。

text: I asked you how many times bees flap their wings per
zh: [51] 我问你蜜蜂每秒钟扇动翅膀多少次,

text: second. You said 190, which was the correct answer. Well done.
zh: [52] 你说是190次,这是正确答案。太棒了。

text: Ooh! OK, let's recap the vocabulary we've learnt, starting with vegan – someone who doesn't consume any animal products.
zh: [53] 哦!好的,让我们来回顾一下今天学到的词汇,首先是vegan——指不食用任何动物制品的人。

text: If you say there's a question mark around something, you mean there's some doubt or or uncertainty about it.
zh: [54] 如果你说某事“there's a question mark around it”,意思是对这件事存在疑虑或不确定。

text: If an animal species is clinging on, it's holding onto existence with great effort.
zh: [55] 如果某个动物物种正在“clinging on”,意味着它正靠着极大的努力勉强维持生存。

text: Isolated pockets refer to small areas of habitat in which a particular insect or animal can survive.
zh: [56] Isolated pockets(孤立栖息地)指的是某种昆虫或动物能够生存的小片栖息区域。

text: To take something for granted means to not appreciate or show gratitude for something which helps you.
zh: [57] To take something for granted 意思是对帮助过你的事物不加珍惜或不表示感激。

text: And finally, the adjective oblivious means
zh: [58] 最后,形容词oblivious的意思是

text: unaware. Once again, our six minutes are up, but if you're keen for more,
zh: [59] 浑然不觉、毫无意识。我们的六分钟又到了,但如果你还想学更多,

text: then head over to our website bbclearningenglish .com and download the worksheet and try the quiz on this episode.
zh: [60] 欢迎前往我们的网站bbclearningenglish.com,下载练习题,试试本期的小测验。

text: Goodbye for now. Goodbye. 6 Minute English From bbclearningenglish .com
zh: [61] 再见啦。再见。6分钟英语 来自bbclearningenglish.com
